name: Fraction of a Quantity — Sharing a Whole Group
whatIsIt: Finding a fraction OF a group means splitting the group into equal parts and taking some of them. To find 1/3 of 12, split 12 into 3 equal parts (12 ÷ 3 = 4) and take one part. For 2/3 of 12, take two of those parts (4 + 4 = 8): divide by the bottom, multiply by the top.
whyNeeded: Real sharing is almost always a fraction of a group, not of one object: half of the class, a quarter of your pocket money, two-thirds of the marbles. This is also the bridge to percentages and to multiplying fractions.
realLifeUses: Half of a class of 30 children going on a trip
Spending a quarter of ₹40 pocket money
Two-thirds of a box of 12 mangoes being ripe
A recipe needing three-quarters of a 200 g pack

vocabulary.meaning: A fraction applied to a whole group, e.g., 1/4 of 20.
In fraction problems, 'of' signals splitting the group into equal parts.
A fraction with 1 on top, like 1/3 — one equal part.
Divide by the bottom number, then multiply by the top: n ÷ b × t.

story.title: The Mango Seller's Ripe Basket
story.text: In the busy summer market, Ravi eyes a basket of 12 golden alphonso mangoes. 'Two-thirds are ripe and sweet today,' calls the mango seller. 'How many is that?' Ravi wonders aloud. The seller tips the mangoes into 3 equal little piles — that is thirds — 4 mangoes in each pile. 'One-third is 4 mangoes. Two-thirds is TWO of those piles: 4 and 4 make 8 ripe mangoes.' Ravi happily buys all 8.
story.extractedProblem: A basket has 12 mangoes and two-thirds are ripe. How many mangoes are ripe?
story.answerInStory: 8 mangoes — split 12 into 3 groups of 4, then take 2 groups (4 + 4 = 8).

visual.caption: 12 split into 3 equal groups of 4. One-third = 4; two-thirds = 8.

teachingGallery.title: Fraction of a group
teachingGallery.note: Divide the group by the bottom (equal shares), then take the top number of shares.

examples.caption: 1/4 of 12: split 12 into 4 groups of 3 → 1 group = 3.
3/4 of 24: quarters of 24 = 6, three of them = 18.

standardMethod.summary: To find a fraction of a group: divide the group by the bottom number to make equal parts, then multiply by the top number to take that many.
steps: Split the whole group into equal parts: divide by the BOTTOM number.
That gives the size of ONE part (a unit fraction of the group).
Multiply that part by the TOP number to take that many parts.

alternateMethods.name: Draw-and-share
alternateMethods.whenToUse: When first learning, or for small groups.
steps: Draw the whole group as dots.
Circle them into equal groups (the bottom number of groups).
Count the dots in as many groups as the top number.
alternateMethods.example: 3/4 of 8: make 4 groups of 2, take 3 groups → 6.

alternateMethods.name: Unit-fraction first
alternateMethods.whenToUse: For any fraction of a quantity.
steps: Find the unit fraction first (1 over the bottom).
Top number is 1, so take one part.
Top number is 3 — take three parts.

trickPractice.trick: Divide by the bottom, times the top
trickPractice.intro: 'Of' a group = split into bottom-many shares, then take top-many of them.

commonMistakes.mistake: Multiplying by the bottom number instead of dividing (20 × 4 for 1/4 of 20).
commonMistakes.fix: For a fraction OF a group, DIVIDE by the bottom to make the equal parts first. 1/4 of 20 = 20 ÷ 4 = 5, not 20 × 4.

commonMistakes.mistake: Finding one part but forgetting to multiply by the top number (giving 5 for 3/4 of 20).
commonMistakes.fix: After dividing by the bottom, MULTIPLY by the top. 3/4 of 20: 20 ÷ 4 = 5, then × 3 = 15.

commonMistakes.mistake: Giving the whole group as the answer, forgetting to take only the fraction.
commonMistakes.fix: A fraction of a group is only PART of it (unless the fraction is 1 whole). 1/3 of 12 is 4, not 12.

hintLadder: Take the top number of parts: 2.

teachBackPrompt: Grab a handful of buttons. Teach someone at home how to find two-thirds of them: split into equal groups (divide by the bottom), then take the top number of groups. Explain why you divide, not multiply, by the bottom.

revisionCard.summary: Fraction OF a group: DIVIDE by the bottom to make equal parts, then MULTIPLY by the top to take them. 3/4 of 20 = 20 ÷ 4 × 3 = 15.

gameMission.title: Two-Thirds of the Mangoes
gameMission.brief: Share whole baskets fairly — split into equal groups and take the right fraction each time.

realLifeProject: Count your crayons or coins. Find half of them, then a quarter of them, by making equal groups. Check your answers by counting.

rememberIt.hook: 'of' means multiply. Fraction of an amount: divide by the bottom, times the top
rememberIt.unpack: 3/4 of 20: divide by 4 (=5), times 3 (=15). Bottom = how many pieces, top = how many you take.

studentQuestions.a: Divide by the bottom (20/4 = 5), then multiply by the top (5 x 3 = 15).

studentQuestions.q: What do the top and bottom mean?
studentQuestions.a: Bottom (denominator) = how many equal pieces the whole is cut into. Top (numerator) = how many of those pieces you take.
